steps.stdoutTail:
run a task in agent mode (default for free-form input)
  xr ask                                 answer a question without tools (read-only, cheap)
  xr plan                                produce a step-by-step plan without executing tools
  xr research                            source-first research with citable reports
  xr agents                              multi-agent supervisor workflows
  xr control                             safe desktop and browser computer control
  xr voice                               voice input/output (opt-in)

  Context
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  xr workspace                           list, create, switch, or delete workspaces
  xr session                             list, inspect, or export past sessions
  xr memory                              durable, inspectable, user-controlled memory
  xr config                              view or update configuration

  Intelligence
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  xr providers                           manage LLM providers — list, set primary model, add keys, test
  xr models                              local models — recommend, install, list, set, test (change model anytime)
  xr budget                              view and set spend caps

  Extensions
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  xr skills                              unified skills runtime + marketplace
  xr plugins                             discover, install, enable, and manage plugins
  xr mcp                                 Model Context Protocol servers

  Trust & safety
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  xr shield                              local security, privacy, and system integrity
  xr audit                               tamper-evident audit log: tail, verify, export
  xr attacks                             run prompt-injection defense benchmark

  System
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  xr doctor                              full system health check + repair hints
  xr status                              installation and component status
  xr update                              check for updates and install with rollback guard
  xr repair                              repair config, permissions, and dependencies safely
  xr logs                                show recent XR runtime / audit activity

  Global flags
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  --help, -h                           show help
  --version, -v                        print version
  --json                               machine-readable JSON on stdout
  --yaml                               YAML output (where supported)
  --format text|json|yaml|markdown     output format
  --quiet, -q                          suppress non-essential output
  --verbose                            extra detail
  --debug                              debug mode (also XR_DEBUG=1)
  --no-color                           disable ANSI color (also NO_COLOR=1)
  --yes, -y                            assume yes for confirmations
  --workspace <id>                     target workspace
  --mode agent|plan|ask                execution mode
  --model <name>                       model override
  --provider <id>                      provider override
  --budget <usd>                       per-task spend ceiling
  --dry-run                            simulate without side effects

  Modes  (same in Shell · Control Center · CLI)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  agent  execute tools, write files, run shell (approvals enforced)
  plan   produce a plan only — no tool execution
  ask    answer-only, read-only, cheap

  Topic help:  xr help <topic>
  Topics:  shell  providers  models  memory  security  skills  research  workspace  budget  scripting

  Docs     https://github.com/ahmadrrrtx/xr
  Website  https://xr-gules.vercel.app

  › A user should not need docs for common tasks — try xr doctor and xr "hello".

Required baseline must not depend on cloud credentials or network.

knownLimitations: This validation run is Linux-only unless a Windows/macOS runner executes the same command.
Cloud providers and local model servers are discovery/health-checked but not required without user credentials/runtimes.
Performance numbers are produced by scripts/measure-baseline.ts and are host-specific.
